_comment: OUR OWN content (NOT ROM-derived) — safe to commit. Defines which sprite groups are hostile and the enemy spawners. Each spawner owns a fixed pool of enemy slots, appended after the extracted NPCs on BOTH client and server so wire ids stay aligned. Slots start inactive (hp 0 = hidden); the server activates them at the spawn point over time only while a player is within ACTIVE_RADIUS, they roam within wanderRadius of the spawn point, and on death they return to the pool to be re-activated. Anchors sit on Onett's connected outdoor street network (verified via tools/plan_shark_spawns.mjs flood fill) so roamers can actually reach the roads. Sprite 284 = Sharks gang. Changing this file needs a dev-server restart (npcSim reads it once at startup) and a client refresh.

spawners.name: onett-arcade-sharks
spawners._note: The game building (arcade). Its door is at (1592,1684) but doorway tiles are solid (you can't stand on them) and the doorstep is collision-sealed from the road, so sharks spawn at the closest standable street cell in front of the door — verified via tools/plan_shark_spawns.mjs. Wide roam so the Sharks spill out of the arcade and run wild all over town.
